codeReview.summary: The fix commit correctly addresses the original blocking bug by removing init.rs-local shell detection and wiring cmd_init to the shared shell::detect_shell() path (which includes parent-process fallback). However, the new regression test intended to verify SHELL-unset fallback is ineffective and cannot fail, so the requested regression protection is not actually implemented.

issues.description: The regression test `test_detect_shell_works_without_shell_env` uses a tautological assertion (`result.is_some() || result.is_none()`), which always passes and does not verify parent-process fallback behavior when SHELL is unset. This leaves the feature requirement ('add regression test verifying fallback') unmet.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill prescribes TDD (write failing tests first), but this fix session implemented code changes before adding/updating tests while still reporting `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`; procedure compliance criteria appear unclear in shared practice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md step 3 requires red-first TDD. Transcript skeleton for session abedec3d-75c0-4655-a045-9adcea666bfb shows Edit on src/commands/init.rs implementation before the later test-edit step, then reports followedProcedure=true in handoff.

summary: Fail: the original shell-detection wiring issue from fix-cli-foundation-scrutiny is fixed in code, but the added regression test is non-assertive and does not validate the SHELL-unset parent-process fallback requirement.
